channel.title: Philosophy on OranLooney.com
channel.description: Recent content in Philosophy on OranLooney.com

item.title: Why the Monty Hall Problem Drives People Crazy
item.description: This essay isn’t to explain the solution to the Monty Hall problem—you can look that up anywhere—but to ask a related question: why does it seem to drive some people crazy? Why do they get so attached to their wrong answers, and so upset by the correct answer? That’s weird, right?

item.title: Eight Billion People
item.description: Today is the last day when the number of people alive will start with a seven. Sometime late Tuesday afternoon, or perhaps early Wednesday morning, the population will cross the eight billion mark. When I was a kid, the number they taught us in school was five billion. By the time I was in college, it was up to six, and a decade ago it hit seven.

item.title: Semantic Code
item.description: se-man-tic (si-man’tik) adj.     1. Of or relating to meaning, especially meaning in language. Programming destroys meaning. When we program, we first replace concepts with symbols and then replace those symbols with arbitrary codes — that’s why it’s called coding. At its worst programming is write-only: the program accomplishes a task, but is incomprehensible to humans.
